retry.notes: This scene has NO EYES anywhere — that is expected and correct, not a problem with the image. Do not look for eyes. The round double rings are PORTHOLE WINDOWS on a space station, and every window interior stays pure BLACK and empty. In THIS drawing there are no naturally-white features either, so fill NOTHING solid white: whiten no region at all. Just redraw the drawing as thin white chalk outlines — keep every enclosed interior black and empty, with no pupils, no scleras, no catchlights, no highlight dots, no glints, and no new marks of any kind anywhere.

chalk.review: Fixed 2026-07-14: the 2026-07 wave chalk had INVENTED a solid pupil-disc-with-catchlight inside ALL FOUR windows (three head portholes + the big body hatch, pupils glancing sideways). The regen took 3 takes and the invention MIGRATED twice, both takes gate-green (invented 0.0000): a windows-are-not-eyes + ban-all-new-marks note still got 'lens glint' catchlight dots in every window (white 0.1%), and adding an explicit empty-interiors ban at t 0.2 got the INVERSE — whole window interiors whitened into eyeballs with black pupil dots, plus white discs in the side thruster ports (white 0.6%). What cleared it (take 3, first attempt): the house-wide-style NO-EYES opener plus 'fill NOTHING solid white: whiten no region at all' — on this page the correct chalk whitens zero regions, so ban whitening wholesale instead of listing banned features. Confirm every window is empty on the chalk-over-night COMPOSITE after any regen; every gate passes an invented face.
chalk.why: 2026-07-14 ISSUES Tier 1 fix (chalk-invented facial features), part 2 — the eye-flavored chalk instruction maps double-ring windows onto its sclera/pupil/catchlight recipe. The eye audit is light-referenced (0 cores), so the invented eyes were invisible to it.

retry.notes: Keep the station's daytime identity colors, dimmed for night: silver-grey hull modules, deep blue checkerboard solar panels, muted red side ports and small red dots, soft green circles on the lower module, warm cream stars, and a blue-and-green Earth below. All four round windows (the three small head portholes and the large central hatch) are plain WINDOWS, not eyes: fill each one with a single plain warm amber glass color — do NOT paint pupils, eyeballs, irises, or catchlight discs inside any of them. Every hull band and panel stays one flat plain color: do NOT add rows of dots, lights, rivets, or ANY new marks or shapes anywhere in the drawing — paint only regions the line art draws. Draw crisp WHITE lines only — do NOT add dark outlines, rims, or drop shadows beside the white lines.

night.review: Conditions on the 2026-07-14 empty-window chalk. Confirm on the composite: all four windows plain amber glass (no pupils), no invented dot rows on any hull band. The fill inventing INSIDE the subject is live on this page: the first pinned take painted a row of red rivet-dots on the head's dome band, and a later take (with the dot ban in place) migrated the same dots to the central module's bands — reject any take with decorative dots the pen doesn't draw. Shipped take: drift 0.0004, bgLuma 26, lineW 255, shipped haloScore 2.068 — that score is the deliberate mid-grey window ring bands (the audit's documented benign class, rawScore 2.1, clean at 1×), matching the light fill's silver ring bands; takes that paint the ring bands deep navy score ~0.7 but this one was the only invention-free take.
night.why: 2026-07-14 ISSUES Tier 1 fix, part 2 — night regenerated against the de-eyed chalk; palette pinned to the light identity per the ISSUES night-contrast trap.

retry.notes: The porthole is a WINDOW, not an eye — the pen has an empty riveted ring and the light fill shows plain glass with a sheen. Do NOT draw a pupil, eyeball, or catchlight disc inside it; leave the window interior empty. There are NO eyes and NO face anywhere in this drawing — that is expected and correct. Do NOT invent ANY new marks anywhere in the drawing: no new dots, discs, circles, blobs, or shapes of any kind, inside or outside any outline. Trace ONLY the strokes that already exist in the drawing.

chalk.review: Fixed 2026-07-14: the 2026-07 wave chalk had drawn a solid pupil-disc-with-catchlight inside the empty porthole and the night fill painted it as a literal brown eyeball — and audit-fill-eyes scored that invented eyeball 8/8 lively cores, all gates green, so the eye machinery actively REWARDED the defect. The seeded window-not-eye note extended with the ban-all-new-marks clause cleared it first take (keep 100%, white 0.0%). The 'eye whites not chalked (3)' warning is expected and correct on the fixed chalk — the light-referenced cores are the porthole glass sheen, which deliberately stays unchalked. Confirm the porthole is an empty ring on the chalk-over-night COMPOSITE after any regen.
chalk.why: 2026-07-14 ISSUES Tier 1 fix (chalk-invented facial features), part 2 — the eye-flavored chalk instruction invents eyes on faceless subjects (see objects/house-wide's NO-EYES guard); regenerated chalk + night, re-punched.

retry.notes: Keep the rocket's daytime identity colors, dimmed for night: deep warm red body, dimmed golden-yellow nose cone, muted teal fins and base band, warm orange flame, soft grey-lavender smoke clouds, warm cream stars. The porthole is a plain WINDOW, not an eye: keep its rim ring a muted steel-blue and paint the round glass interior one plain dim glass color — do NOT paint a pupil, eyeball, iris, or catchlight disc inside it, and do NOT invent any new marks or shapes anywhere in the drawing. The big front smoke cloud is CROPPED by the bottom of the page: paint its grey all the way down to the bottom page edge exactly as the lines show — do NOT close it with a new bottom outline and do NOT leave sky below it. Draw crisp WHITE lines only — do NOT add dark outlines, rims, or drop shadows beside the white lines.

night.review: Conditions on the 2026-07-14 empty-porthole chalk. Confirm on the composite: porthole plain steel-blue glass (no eyeball), identity colors matching the light fill dimmed. TWO model habits burned most of a 10-take budget here, and the retry notes carry the counter for each: (1) without '-t 0.4 + crisp WHITE lines only' it re-inks thin dark rims beside every white line (haloScore 13-17 vs the shipped 0.026 — this page shipped the catalog's worst halo, 8.5, in the 2026-07 wave); (2) flat takes systematically CLOSE the page-cropped front cloud with an invented white bottom arc (drift 0.0039-0.0041, right at the 0.004 bar — would freeze as the catalog's first night driftOk FAIL); the cloud-is-cropped sentence cleared it in one take (drift 0.0012, haloScore 0.018).
night.why: 2026-07-14 ISSUES Tier 1 fix, part 2 — night regenerated against the de-eyed chalk; halo history 8.5 (shipped 2026-07, catalog #1) -> 0.026. Palette pinned to the light identity per the ISSUES night-contrast trap.
